expected: G launches the luminous gravity tracer, H launches the Higgs bomb (both gated by flight/landed as designed); unknown action names in onAction must fail loudly or auto-register, never silently drop handlers.
notes: Fix in space-war-true-proportions-v1 rc-2: onAction auto-creates lists ((listeners[name] ??= []).push) killing the silent-drop class; new e2e uses REAL KeyG/KeyH keypresses (lesson: debug-call coverage is not input coverage).
repro: Take off, press G or H: nothing happens, no toast. Press F: nuke fires (nuke key exists in the map). Shipped in space-war-physics-fidelity-v1: tests and live verification exercised __swDebug.launchGravBomb()/launchHiggs() directly - only the nuke was verified via a real keypress.
symptom: Operator: of the 3 weapons only nukes work; the Higgs bomb does nothing and projectiles are never attracted by gravity wells (wells never spawn since [H] is dead). Root cause: input.js listeners map is a fixed literal without gravbomb/higgs keys; onAction uses listeners[name]?.push(fn) which silently no-ops for unknown names, so main.js handlers for gravbomb/higgs are never registered and emit() finds no listeners.
title: [G] tracer and [H] Higgs bomb keyboard actions never fire - listeners map lacks their entries, onAction silently no-ops
